sections.paragraph:
εἰρήναρχος, ου, ὁ (εἰρήνη, ἄρχομαι; IMilet I/7, 263, 7; IGR IV, 1543; SEG XLI, 1044, 5; POxy 1507, 1; 1662, 19; PAm 139, 3f and 11 [350 a.d.]; 146, 2 [V a.d.]) a magistrate of imperial times, chief of police, police captain, MPol 6:2; 8:2.—Lghtf. ad loc.; WLiebenam, Städteverwaltung im röm. Kaiserreich 1900, 358.—S. DELG s.v. ἄρχω C p. 120.

IGR IGR = Inscriptiones Graecae ad res Romanas pertinentes—List 3

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 287
